turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: We'll hear argument now in Number oh oh seventeen fifty-one, Susan Tave Zelman, Superintendent of Public Instruction of Ohio versus Doris Simmons-Harris, and two related cases. Ms. French. [Inaudible]

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court. In nineteen ninety-five, the Ohio General Assembly responded to an unprecedented educational crisis by enacting the Ohio Scholarship and Tutorial Program. Under this Court's decisions, especially Mueller, Witters, and Zobrest, and in light of this Court's teachings, most recently in Agostini and Mitchell, the Ohio program is constitutional because it offers a neutral program that offers true private choice to parents. First, the principle of neutrality. There are two criteria that determine where the benefits will go under the program. First is residence in a school district that is or has been taken over by State control. Second is family income. Neither of these criteria has anything to do with religion, but even beyond these basic...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: elements... Well, do you you don't take the...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: position that that guarantees constitutionality, do you?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: We do not, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: We ha- have a two-pronged approach and that...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: W- t- so, you take it as a necessary condition, but not a sufficient condition?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: We do, Your Honor.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: And you think it would be unconstitutional if it didn't have that...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Not necessarily, Your Honor, but it certainly goes to the neutrality of the program,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: but even beyond the nondiscrim-

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: matter? I mean, if- if they're proselytizing, doesn't it make good sense for them to admit anybody who may come along, and yet the proselytizing can't be subsidized under the Establishment Clause?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: We, of course, Your Honor, do not agree that they're proselytizing.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: and perhaps I should move to the second prong, then, and talk about-

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: An- and talk about the true private choice that is at issue here for the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: parents.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: I- I take it that the first part of your argument as demonstrated is to try to show that there are certain in- indexes, indicia of neutrality.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Correct.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: and you tick them off.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Third and...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: percentage of the students in the school system are get vouchers?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Well, there are fifty-seven thousand students elementary students in the in the in the Cleveland School District, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: scholarships.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: many thousand get scholarships, two thousand?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: I- in nineteen ninety-nine the number was thirty-seven hundred.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: It's now about forty-four Yes, Your Honor, a little less than ten percent,

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: but all the students in the s- Cleveland School District are eligible.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Schools...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: you a question about private choice which is a very important part of the case? Supposing you had a situation with a small community that had one public school and one religious school, and they would pay for the voucher to go to the religious school if the family, on its own private choice, wanted to go to the re- the child to go to the religious school.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I think it would, Your Honor given i- i- if of course, this...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: irrelevant, really that there're four or five choices available, as long as there's a free choice either to go to the public school or to go to the religious school? Well, we have a number of choices within the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: traditional public schools, Your Honor. They are...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: not necessary, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: his whole point...

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: there's such a dramatic difference between a choice from the great universe of colleges and universities, what a particular student will choose, and here, the difference you just explained to Justice Stevens that maybe it doesn't matter.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: That's currently true, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: true...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: why we don't take account of the availability of the community schools in analyzing this program? We would like the Court to take very much account of the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: community schools, Your Honor. That's correct, Your Honor. The...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: in fact, to the parents?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Very much so, Your Honor.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: And the tuition assistance would be provided if the selection were for a community school?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: There would be no tuition assistance, Your Honor, only because they are public schools, so there's no need for a scholarship there Parents can choose a traditional public school, they can choose a tutoring grant if they're in a if they're in a public school, they can choose a magnet school, they can choose a community school, or...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: And if- if a community school is selected, no additional money then is provided, as would be provided i- if the religious school were selected? That's true, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It's only if...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: have some of the private nonsectarian schools in the city become community schools?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: They have, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Are there...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Because they get more...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: money. That's that's the...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: more money, and in part because of the uncertainty of the litigation.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Are slots available in the community schools for these children that we're talking about?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, there are spots...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: available.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Available in both...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: community schools...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: tutoring grant if you go to a community school?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, you can.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Is there anything in the record about the quality of these community schools? There was one brief that said they were too new, too few, too unregulated, too untested to count.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: I would direct the Court to two places in the record, particularly the joint appendix.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: for them.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: there a description of the precise community schools that are par- participating in the program, and the quality of education in those schools?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: There is to to the first part of your question, Your Honor, and that's in Mr. Puckett's affidavit, of just describing what the schools are, why we have them in Ohio.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Is there information in the record about the quality of the religiously affiliated schools?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: There're there are a number of studies that have been done both in Cleveland and with respect to other pro- other scholarship pr- programs, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: appendix and that...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: particular schools in this program.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, in general the scholarship program, not just specifically the religious schools, but the voucher er the scholarship program as a whole,

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Hm.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: as to whether the students are showing academic achievement or, you know, significant results beyond that.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Just, before we leave the community schools, when the State calculates the funding that goes to the community schools, it takes account of the number of students that go to the community schools, I take it.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Oh, yes, Your Honor,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: it's...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: figure of something like five thousand dollars...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: per student not quite that.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Does the same amount of money per capita go to a community school as would go to the regular public school...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: annually?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: approximately.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: May I ask you if this Court would have to overrule the Nyquist case to support your position?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It certainly...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: points the other way, doesn't it?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: It does point the other way, Your Honor, but we think that there are a number of distinctions which this Court has drawn between the programs at issue, say, in Mueller and Witters that distinguish it from the New York program at issue in Nyquist.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: which can...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: concern about the Establishment Clause?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Well, this Court has pointed to specifically footnote thirty-eight, where the Court reserved judgment in the Nyquist decision for programs that offered a benefit, the specific example was scholarships there, and offered to a broad base of beneficiaries without regard to the nonpublic or public or nonsectarian, sectarian nature of the institutions benefited which is precisely precisely what is happening here.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Well, but doesn't that simply then go back to this neutrality point, and you're saying because it's neutral, in the sense that it's offered in an even-handed way, query I'm me- your your friends on the other tide dispute that, but just accepting that categorization, because it's neutral in that sense, that's a distinction which ought to make a difference in the result.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: We, of course, disagree, Your Honor, that there is a massive amount going to religious schools as a result of something that the Government is doing.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: we were on...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: term as result of what the Government is doing, which is a separate issue as to whether what the significance is of the private choice, but the the effect that Nyquist was concerned with, and the effect that I think has been shown here, is a substantial amount of money, A to the schools themselves, and in relation to the amount of money spent on the program,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and in those...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: respects the the two are identical.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Well, in that respect, Your Honor, there's no question that there is money that is ending up in religious institutions, because that's what the what the parents have chosen, but that nondiscrimination provision that I spoke of earlier did not exist in Nyquist.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: to all comers.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: also made the point, I think, that where the parents do the choosing, as they did not do in Nyquist, it was a different ball game.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Absolutely, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that rule...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: closest of our cases, do you think, to the Ohio program? Is it Witters?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: I would suggest Witters, Your Honor, because it's a financial aid going to there it was a college student, but an adult, to make a decision about where to send the money.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The difference would be...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: The difference would be however that according to respondents the choices are much more limited here...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: than in...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Witters.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: That's true, Your Honor, but in Mueller the Court did address that concern, as Justice Powell said in his concurrence in Witters that it didn't matter that there was only one person, Mr. Witters, using the money for seminary, for the Inland Empire School of the Bible, nor did it matter in Zobrest that there was only one child or one parent set of parents for a child looking for an interpretive or religious school.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: part of the design of the program is to have a structure which will encourage over the long term more and different kinds of school choices,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: in- i- including,

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: of course the community schools...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Absolutely, Your Honor.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: suggestion that in Nyquist, it's a difference when the parents do the choosing, well who chose where the schoo- children would go to school in Nyquist? Didn't the parents de- make the decision?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: The parents, of course, did,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: same case.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: I disagree, Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Very well, Ms. French.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, may it please the Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and  Nyquist.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: the money went to families with children in nonpublic schools, but that's exactly what's happening here.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor, if...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: just for people in the nonpublic schools?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, it isn't exactly the same at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Nyquist...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: that the vouchers are just for people in nonpublic schools?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: In this...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: case, no.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, we have tutorial vouchers for people in in public schools, and tutorial vouchers for magnet schools and community...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: schools.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: tutorial vouchers, why is the number of tutorial vouchers limited to the same number of vouchers for paid to the private schools?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Well, I would s- Your Honor, I would suspect the answer to that is to try to provide some form of equality and and to make sure that there was no Government endorsement of one choice or another, so the e- equality of having the same number of grants for for tutoring being the same as the same number going for scholarships.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Of course, the amount of money is vastly different, isn't it, because the I forget the figures exactly, but isn't the the limit on the tutorial something like three hundred and fifty dollars a student, as opposed to the two thousand some odd limit on the on the tuition vouchers?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, there is a difference, but there is less a difference than the difference between the public school and the nonpublic school deductions taken in Mueller.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Well, you wouldn't limit it to the vouchers anyway, would you? I mean, you would you would think that we have to look at the money that goes to the community schools.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Th- that is ab-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: go via bou- vouchers, but goes directly to the schools, and it's a greater amount of money that goes to the private schools, isn't it?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, I think the fact there is no question that when this program was initially implemented, every single secular school in the district signed up to participate.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: what is actually bother- i- I'd like to hear what you say about the endorsement point that Justice Souter initially raised, and my thought is, I'll assume no discrimination, and I'll assume it's a fine program, but imagine you came from Europe or Africa, or a different place, and said, what do they do in the United States by way of educating their children, and you're told, well, sixty billion dollars a year, forty billion, or some very large amount of money is being spent by the Government to give children K through twelve what is basically a religiously oriented education taught by a parochial school.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Thank you, Your Honor.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: There's an irony, I g- are you is that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I could go on, Your Honor, but...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: No, if if I mean, the irony is that the better the parochial school, in a sense, the less the freedom of choice.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: most of th-

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: mean, I I no one's complaining about the quality of of of the program.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: For the reasons I've already noted, Your Honor, I believe there is no governmental no governmental endorsement, and you have to realize that i- th- th- the overwhelming majority of the elible [: eligible] eligible children elected to remain in the public school, and incidentally...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: there are states...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Justice Breyer could send his child to one of the community schools, which is entirely nonsectarian, under this program, right?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Y- Y- Your Honor, that's another alternative, and I think we...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Which schools would get more money than the than the th- than the sectarian schools anyway.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, I see that as another reason why no one could say there's a reasonable message of governmental endorsement in this case.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: You you agree that the Sixth Circuit erred.

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, the same approach was taken by Judge Oliver, but I d- don't feel that the Sixth Circuit really understood how the community school program worked, or how one could use the tutorial vouchers to help the children that elected to go to the community schools.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: Well, there's really no record on the community schools, you're saying, because you weren't permitted to make a record?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, there is an extensive record fr- of in affidavits in terms of the creation of the of the of the community schools, the transfer of the two secular scholarship schools to community school status.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Do we have to link the two programs together to resolve the case?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor, I believe not.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: trogram [: program] and the money paid to the parents and endorsed over to the schools in the case of choice? Do we have to consider both together?

turns.name: DAVID L. YOUNG
turns.text: Your Honor, I would consider them together, but it was it's the all of the indicia of choice, not just the endorsement.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Young.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: No but I think you would say the program was still constitutional, even if it was just conceived in the healthiest school system in the world.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, perhaps, Justice Stevens, I might, but this does i- this th- as this Court has taught repeatedly, the background history and the context informs the decision which this Court has endorsed with respect to what the effects or endorsement test would be, measured by what a reasonable, objective observer would believe the State or the Government was doing.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: suggests...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: that argument is, why did they make th- this wonderful solution available to such a small percentage of the student body?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What what I w- I would d- invite the Court's attention to page forty-one of the Taylor petitioner's brief, which contains a chart which shows the various choices which were made available to the students as a result of the composite, the context of the program that we're considering.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: attention...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I I didn't I didn't quite understand Justice Stevens' question.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: students?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I I...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: What I meant...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: program was available to all the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: students.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I I stand corrected.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: students.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: could have gone into into a one of the community schools, or to one of the private schools,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: isn't that right?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: an- and th- and the record is quite clear on this, also.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: school.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: there doesn't seem to be a record on this very clear, that my impression was really the parochial schools are an awful lot better.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: but but...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: to send the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: case back? Does it turn on?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: No, no.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Well if la- mi- mi- General Olson, if if a private indiv- individual s- challenges a State law as unconstitutional, the burden of proof is on that individual, isn't it, to m- show the necessary facts to establish unconstitutionality?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes, Mr. Chief Justice, absolutely, but the record here in goes even further than that, because the record that is available shows these many alternatives.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: available.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: to ask how the courts faced with this challenge have to view the case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes, Justice O'Connor, I believe that that is the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: correct context.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: below not...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: do that?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: court made a m- a legal error in failing to do so, because this Court has taught over and over again that the context is extremely...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: important in history.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: only to low income children, or does it just i- does that affect the amount of money to be given?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It affects both the amount of money to be given and the preference.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: There's only a finite amount of money available.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, that's always the case, of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: course in any...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Government program, but the priorities are given to the low income people.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the alterna-

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: was no attempt made in the program to make sure that the money that ends up in the parochial schools is not used for religious training, or teaching.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: There there have been other Federal programs, for example, where there have been such limitations on usage.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: here.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that's correct, Justice O'Connor, but the Court has made the made the point in connection with those types of programs that there's a significant difference between a direct aid program, where funds are going from the Government to the school, as opposed to the private cho- p- genuinely independent, purely private choice programs where the choices are being made by individual parents, and meing [: being] being made by individual parents motivated by the best education for their children.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: of choices...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: very question, do you think these alternatives are essential from a constitutional point of view, or would you make the same argument if there were merely the one choice,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: religious school or the public school?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: applying the k- the standards this Court has adopted, that th- the euv- if the n- criteria are neutral and I'm answering I mak- I'm saying...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: yes and tha-

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: You can either go to the public school, or you can go to the parochial school, and if you go to the parochial school, we'll pay the tuition.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Which we're we're also offeri- an- the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the yes. Yes.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: here, but what would you do with that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: case?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: if there was a purely neutral criteria in terms of eligibility for the program, and it's a purely private choice, that the in- that the because this Court has emphasized that we're looking at whether the Government's being going going to be perceived by a reasonable observer as endorsing religion, if it is a purely private choice program, the teaching of this Court is, it's not unlike a Government check that goes to an indiv- a g- an individual who then spends it, all of it on...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: his church.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: purely private.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: which one. And you would...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: say that's perfectly all right.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, I would I would probably be making that argument in another case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: private schools.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: whether or not those alternatives are constitutionally necessary, or just make your argument...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: stronger.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that what this Court has taught, that because these establishment Clause cases are so difficult, that they are made in the context of the particular facts of the case, and that the facts and circumstances in history illuminate what the Government was involved in, because we're not talking about...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: But why why don't we why don't you st- well, I know why you don't stress, but why shouldn't we stress as one of those facts the bottom line of ninety-six percent of the kids taking the the tuition aid, are taking it in in parochial schools?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: doesn't that suggest that there is perhaps something specious about this notion that it's a matter of wide open choice here? In practical terms, the money's going to end up where it ends up, and the ninety-six percent figure is pretty...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: persuasive.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: same factor in the Mueller case, an and and one of the other cases that wer- that wa- that was that has been cited, the Court said that is not of constitutional significance.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: going to...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: asking you a question about practical significance, and and why do we eliminate that fact from our judgment about what in the real world seems to be going...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: on?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: tho- those choices this Court has said are are the result of purely private choices, and that that will not be associated by a reasonable observer with a governmental decision.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, General Olson.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, Mr. Chanin,

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: wait wait wait just a minute.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: The limitation to looking at the voucher program as a freestanding program is consistent both with the precedents of this Court and with absolute logic,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: I don't understand either point, to tell you the truth.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Your Honor, this Court has always been program specific in its financial aid cases.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Schools and New York State.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: proper.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But the question is whether or not...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: The question is whether or not there is neutrality in this program, and it t- s- seems to me that if you ask us to put on blinders, and and not inquire as to what's really happening into Cincinnati, what really was the reason for this, what all of the choices are, that you're asking us to make a decision b- based on on a fictional premise.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Your Honor, I think we're doing precisely the reverse.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: You're asking us to look at part of a reality.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You you you you don't have any problem with that.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: You you you say it would be perfectly okay if it went to a private school.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: saying...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: could not even go to nonsectarian...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: private schools?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: This money could not, in your view, even go to nonsectarian private...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: schools?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: It could, and that would be a rational way for the State to provide for the education of children...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: some in publicly-run...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: schools and some in private schools, but if any of those private schools is a religiously affiliated school, that is a no-no.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: and that, in your view, is neutrality?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, Your Honor, that is not my position.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: option,

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: you must send your child to get a religious education,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that is not...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: the percentage in Mueller was ninety-six percent.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Th- I believe, Your Honor, that this Court, this case is not controlled by Mueller, for the very same reason that Mueller was not controlled by Nyquist.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The Court...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: does not say here, as you put it, that you must go to these religiously affiliated schools.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: ninety-six percent of the students.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: because some of the private schools had become community schools, and is it not true that parents can choose to have their children educated in a community school and, if they do, that school gets more money from the State than if they had chosen the religious school? If anything, it's skewed against the religious...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: schools in terms of...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: public support.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I think there are two parts to your question, if if I may take them in sequence.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: schools.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: doing a very good job of not answering...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: W-

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: O'Connor's question.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Th-

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: well, the answer to it is this, Justice Kennedy.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: point of view of the co-

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Why do you not put the community schools and the magnet schools in the universe of choices? That's the problem I'm having with your argument.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Well,

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Why?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: s- We we do not look at them for two reasons, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: religious education.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: only true if you say the person is reasonable in not looking at all the choices, which include community schools, certainly.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Your Honor, if it extends that way, there is no meaning any more to the concept of genuinely independent and private choice.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Suddenly it changes, and it's not education any more if you're getting it in a religious school.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: why is that? Unless there's an...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: endorsement of religion involved here, I don't see why the fact that some of the money, even most of the money goes to religious schools makes any difference.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Well, because you have a basic proposition that we build our case on, which the Court has a- has adopted, and it is this.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Well suppose it weren't that number.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Not in my mind,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: so th- all this ninety-nine percent doesn't make that much difference.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: It I I focus on it because it makes it clear to the Court, I hope, that this isn't even a close to the line case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: of those cases.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: what is your response if it's fifty fifty and you have hundreds of millions of...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: dollars and but they're...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: saying remember is...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: private. Because?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: of the criteria that this Court used in Witters.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Hm.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: told us why it was...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Mhm.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: It said, it is constitutional because the aid recipients have genuinely independent and private choice, and then the Court went on to say what that meant.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Mhm.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: of which were secular.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: This is not a linedrawing case.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: just be sure I understand your position.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Well, that's clearly unconstitutional...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: if it's ten percent.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Oh, no.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: to what I think Justice Breyer...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: put to me was, there's a choice and...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that the universe...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: I s- if Iu nderstand the case correctly, your view is, if any one school gets the money, it's...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: unconstitutional.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Well, I thought you said...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: yes.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I'm sorry if I if you thought I did not.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Your Honor.  This is...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: schools, nine s- nine nonsectarian, one sectarian?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I think that is a borderline case, but if it's structured this way,

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Well say...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: ninety-nine nonsectarian and one sectarian.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Give us give us give us something that isn't borderline.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: you why you...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Well, I'm I'm really trying to find out what your position is.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: relatively simply.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: religious educ- it's unconstitutional.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: that in this, and the Government says, y- you pick your school.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: We'll send a check direct to the school.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: that's unconstituti-

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: But do I...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: also have to voice...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: constitutional if the hundred percent,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: But I have to know...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: the choice you're giving me as an aid recipient.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: at the other ninety-nine? Ab- absolutely.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: is to have my child go the sectarian...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: school.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: violates the Establishment Clause, in my opinion.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Because why?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Because certainly I can say, without hesitation, nothing broke the circuit between the State and the general coffer of the sectarian school, your aid recipient in your hypothetical had no choice whatsoever.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Nyquist. I-

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Stevens' hypothetical, the State would pay the tuition to the nonsectarian private schools, too?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: W- that wou-

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I think that's Witters.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: So, w- what breaks the circuit in my fifty fifty case? Does you say it doesn't break the circuit, but they're saying, well the petitioners say, we gave the money to the individuals.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: My response to that is, th- if this Court concluded that the words, significant amount, huge array of choices, if the Court concluded, as an abstract proposition, that those standards were met on fifty fifty, I would be most unhappy, but I would conclude that the program was...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: constitutional.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: not the cases, but give me the the rationale.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: The rationale is this.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: case o-

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: does.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: doesn't it?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I would not forget the cases.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: because of the numbers.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: me ask you this.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Your Honor, it's it's a an unreal hypothetical.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Well, it's not, because in effect that's what's happened here.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, it isn't because the val- the everybody...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: The community school gets forty-five hundred dollars a head, and the s- parochial school twenty-five hundred, so if if it were done by a little voucher working that way, then what is your answer?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: It would be no diffe- it would be un- a violation of the Constitution.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Would it?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It it wouldn't be...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: perceived as...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: giving undue help, or endorsing the religious...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: school.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: It's no different than saying, you can take a voucher, you can leave public education and go to a religious school, or you don't limit it to community schools, or, I'll give you money to go to a community school, I'll give you money to go to a magnet school, I'll give you money to go to a traditional school.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: or leaving and going to private school.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: been referring to community schools as public schools.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: They are not private schools, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: simply w-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: schools have to accept all- allcomers?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: There are certain they ha- they cannot discriminate on certain...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: bases. There are a lot of...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: take kids who pass a certain a certain test, a certain entrance exam?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I I'm not sure you...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: can base it on academic achievement.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: public school system...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Pardon?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: It doesn't sound much like the public school system to...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: me.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: it is, Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Well but wha- why why is there a the bright line that you talk about w- which separates community schools from private schools e- and aligns them with o- other than the fact that they're run by the Government? Here, the community schools, as I understand it, are set up because they wanted to get away from the kind of failing system that so many public schools are, and do something different.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Because, Your Honor, if the concept of breaking the circuit is going to have any meaning, you have to draw a line, and the only rational line to draw is between public education...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: and private education.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: you you've said that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well I s-

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: tell members of the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I say it because...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I I didn't mean to interrupt you, Your...

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: You'd better not...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Is it too late?

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: No.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Wi- with that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: explanation.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: this question? Is it true that the group you put on one side of the line, there's no tuition in those?

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: There is no tuition if...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: you stick with those.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: schools, support th- p- th- p- prong by the pu- where the others, there's tuition.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: Can I could I try once again on another example here? The prototype that this Court has set out for us of genuine, independent, and private choice, is a Government employee.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: have to come work...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: for government in the first...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: place. He had all kinds of options. He could have worked everywhere else.

turns.name: RUTH BADER GINSBURG
turns.text: question, because I think we understand the case of th- the Government employee turning over his paycheck to the Salvation Army, or whatever.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: It would make a difference, but I could not answer as to whether it would be constitutional or unconstitutional as far as the program is concerned, until I saw the specifics of that program.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It is what...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: can you tell me ho- how we get from here to there? Here we have a failed inner city school system, and the State says, part of the m- part of the problem with this is is monopoly.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I I...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: The only schools that happen to be there right now are religious schools.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: We-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to start off with, of course they're mostly religious, an- and that that is going to destroy the entire program, so that we can never get from here to there.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: I do not believe, Your Honor, that a crisis in the Cleveland public schools is a license to ignore the mandate of the Establishment Clause, nor do I think it's a mandate to say,

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: we'll ignore it for a while,

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: because in a few...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: years  . I will tell you. No. No. No. I'll tell you just what   do. What the...

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: State of Ohio should do in this specific case is exactly what the Ohio Supreme Court's been telling them to do for ten years to deal with the problems in Cleveland.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Instead b- b-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: seven thousand dollars per child, which is above the average in the rest of the country.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It's a monopoly problem.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: not according to the Ohio Supreme Court.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: kindergarten classes that's how you solve it.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: it's it's very clear to me that Ohio had that option.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: O-

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Scalia put the point that what they're trying to do is have a structure in which different school systems, different curriculums, curriculums that do not inflict terminal boredom on students, can begin to flourish, and the question is, how can they do that in the long term, and you say they cannot do it.

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: No, I say this, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ROBERT H. CHANIN
turns.text: refer you to the amicus brief of the National School Boards Association.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chanin.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Judge Frankel, wasn't that on the ground that it used th- m- the single subject title,

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: rather than the...

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: it had n- nothing to do with that case.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Judge Frankel, you're you're going to tie this into the question presented in this case?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: That was a program designed to rescue economically all right.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: for this reason.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: where we think...

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: it should stay...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Mhm.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: will be an appropriate resolution for the interests of Ohio and its poor children.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Y- your assumption, Judge Frankel, is that the problem is a problem of money.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Is what, Your Honor?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Is a problem of money.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, not only...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: familiar with suggest that that is not the case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: that that Please please...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: let me finish, sir.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: I'm sorry.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: The studies that I'm familiar with say that that the inner city parochial schools, which spend much less per child on education, do a much better job than the public schools that spend much more, so I I just don't think it follows f- that that that once you solve a constitutional problem that will get more money, you're you're you're going to solve the the difficulty that the people of Cleveland found with their public schools.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: Your Honor, there is mostly anecdotal material comparing the kind of job that's done in parochial and secular schools.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Well I don't think it's anecdotal at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: M- with all...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: by by polit- by , you know, educational scholars.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: With deference, Your Honor, I don't think that the difficulties that I'm trying to suggest about the Court's getting into the details of some of the Establishment Clause cases that have been mooted here are avoided by looking what is said to be a comparison between parochial schools and public schools.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: The problem of c- comparative qualities changes quite markedly, and you don't have the same kind of problem.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Frankel, did you make this argument to the court of appeals?

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: rai-

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: how did they deal with it?

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: Well, Your Honor, th- it sort of slipped by but we we made the further point that le- let me put it this way.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: questions.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: On the Establishment Clause, I should add we are as one with our friends here.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Di- did you cross-petition for certiorari in this...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: case?

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: we didn't we didn't think we had any...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: occasion to do that.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: lawsuit, though.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: brought...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: the lawsuit.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: You're...

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: won the lawsuit.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Yeah.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: And we didn't believe I still don't believe we had occasion to do that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: happened...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Frankel, may I may I ask you, has the have the Ohio courts ever passed upon on on this question? I know they they they passed on the on the the single iss- single statement i- issue under the Constitution.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: issue before the Ohio courts when they passed on the single issue m- matter?

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: The Ohio Supreme Court, Your Honor, in what we considered obiter, said it it found consistency with the Establishment Clause, but it had already held its statute unconstitutional on State grounds, so we never could reach that.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: But the fact that it issued the obiter indicated that it was not concerned with the point that you're now making, and that's the highest court of the State.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: I- it was not concerned with...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I didn't hear you, Your Honor.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: point that you that you're now addressing to us.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: No, Your Honor, it it it didn't take proper concern of everything that we thought it it sh- should have looked at.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Frankel.

turns.name: MARVIN E. FRANKEL
turns.text: Thank you, Your Honor.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Ms. French, you have four minutes remaining.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chief Justice, I have four points.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: schools.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: schools public schools...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: in Ohio?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: They are considered public schools, Your Honor.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: They have separate boards?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: They do.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Separate...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: employees?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: They ar- they do have...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And not...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: the same control over content of programs?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: That's right, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: not contro-

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: financed?

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, and it does have the same sorts of financial requirements.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: because it's not they community...

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: schools do not...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: charge tuition, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUDITH L. FRENCH
turns.text: And and in answer to your question earlier to Mr. Chanin, it's open to all-comers.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Ms. French.
